zh.lifeSummary: 當John Bailey於1814年出生在美國南卡羅來納州時，他的父親John T Bailey28歲，母親Jemima Russell41歲。他於1831年7月15日與Elisabeth Land在美國南卡羅來納州結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他於1850年住在美國阿拉巴馬州德卡爾布且於1870年住在美國阿肯色州蘭多夫洛亞諾可 堂史普。他去世於1871年1月7日在美國阿肯色州勞倫斯拉文登 斯普林斯享年57歲，埋葬在美國阿肯色州勞倫斯拉文登 斯普林斯真斯 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Bailey于1814年出生在美国南卡罗来纳州时，他的父亲John T Bailey28岁，母亲Jemima Russell41岁。他于1831年7月15日与Elisabeth Land在美国南卡罗来纳州结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他于1850年住在美国亚拉巴马州德卡爾布且于1870年住在美国阿肯色州蘭多夫洛亞諾可 堂史普。他去世于1871年1月7日在美国阿肯色州勞倫斯拉文登 斯普林斯享年57岁，埋葬在美国阿肯色州勞倫斯拉文登 斯普林斯真斯 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Bailey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status name for a steward or official, from Middle English bailli ‘manager, administrator’ (Old French baillis, from Late Latin baiulivus, an adjectival derivative of baiulus ‘attendant, carrier, porter’).
English: habitational name from Bailey in Little Mitton, Lancashire, named with Old English beg ‘berry’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

English: occasionally a topographic name for someone who lived by the outer wall of a castle, from Middle English (Old French) bailli ‘outer courtyard of a castle’ (Old French bail(le) ‘enclosure’, a derivative of bailer ‘to enclose’). This term became a placename in its own right, denoting a district beside a fortification or wall, as in the case of the Old Bailey in London, which formed part of the early medieval outer wall of the city.

variants.name: Bailly
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Emile, Marcel, Philippe.
French: occupational name for a steward or official, from Old French baillif. Compare Bailey 4.
French: habitational name from (Le) Bailly, the name of several places in the northern part of France.

variants.name: Bale
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived by the outer wall of a castle, Middle English bailli, baile, from Old French bail(le) ‘enclosure’ (see Bailey 3).
Breton (Balé): variant of Balay .
French: unexplained.

variants.name: Baily
meanings: English: variant of   Bailey  .

variants.name: Bayly
meanings: English: variant of   Bailey  .

variants.name: Bayley
meanings: English: variant of   Bailey  .

variants.name: Balay
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Rajesh, Satish.
Altered form of English   Bailey  .
French: habitational name from any of various minor places so named.

variants.name: Baile
meanings: Spanish: status name for a steward or official, from Old Spanish baile, Late Latin baiulivus; cognate with English Bailey .
French (southern): variant of   Bail  .
English: variant of   Bale  .

variants.name: Baillie
meanings: Scottish:

from Older Scots baillie, baili. In Scotland, a baillie was a status name for any of various officials, including the chief magistrate in a barony and (later) the chief magistrate or administrative officer of a barony or district. Etymologically, this is the same word as English Bailey , but the functions as well as the spelling of the two officials were different in England and Scotland.
topographic name from Older Scots bailli ‘outer court of a castle’; see Bailey 3 and Bale 1.

variants.name: Baley
meanings: English: variant of   Bailey  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.description: Historical Boundaries - 1815: Lawrence, Missouri Territory, United States; 1819: Lawrence, Arkansas Territory, United States; 1836: Lawrence, Arkansas, United States

historicEvents.title: Slave Rebellion
historicEvents.description: "On June 16, 1822,  Denmark Vesey a free and self-educated African American leads a slave rebellion called ""the rising."" The interesting thing about this rebellion is that it does not really happen. The only thing the judges have to go on is the testimony of people that witness it."

historicEvents.title: The Second Great Awakening
historicEvents.description: Being a second spiritual and religious awakening, like the First Great Awakening, many Churches began to spring up from other denominations. Many people began to rapidly join the Baptist and Methodist congregations. Many converts to these religions believed that the Awakening was the precursor of a new millennial age.

historicEvents.title: Angry Mob Seizes U-S Mail
historicEvents.description: On August 31, 1835, in Charleston, South Carolina an angry mob takes control over the U-S mail and burns it in public.
U.S. acquires vast tracts of Mexican territory in wake of Mexican War including California and New Mexico.
In 1860, South Carolina quit the United States because its citizens were in favor of slavery and President Lincoln was not. The Civil War started a year later.
Arkansas supplied an estimated 50,000 men to the Confederate Army andabout 15,000 to the Union Army.

en.lifeSummary: When John Bailey was born in 1814, in South Carolina, United States, his father, John T Bailey, was 28 and his mother, Jemima Russell, was 41. He married Elisabeth Land on 15 July 1831, in South Carolina, United States. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 7 daughters. He lived in DeKalb, Alabama, United States in 1850 and Roanoke Township, Randolph, Arkansas, United States in 1870. He died on 7 January 1871, in Ravenden Springs, Lawrence, Arkansas, United States, at the age of 57, and was buried in Janes Cemetery, Ravenden Springs, Lawrence, Arkansas, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Bailey est né en 1814, à Caroline du Sud, États-Unis, son père, John T Bailey, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Jemima Russell, avait 41 ans. Il a épousé Elisabeth Land le 15 juillet 1831, à Caroline du Sud, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 7 filles. Il a vécu à DeKalb, Alabama, États-Unis en 1850 et à Roanoke Township, Randolph, Arkansas, États-Unis en 1870. Il est décédé le 7 janvier 1871, à Ravenden Springs, Lawrence, Arkansas, États-Unis, à l’âge de 57 ans, et a été enterré à Janes Cemetery, Ravenden Springs, Lawrence, Arkansas, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Bailey 1814 in South Carolina, Vereinigte Staaten geboren wurde, war sein Vater John T Bailey 28 und seine Mutter Jemima Russell 41. Er hat am 15. Juli 1831 in South Carolina, Vereinigte Staaten Elisabeth Land geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 7 Töchter. 1850 hat er in DeKalb, Alabama, Vereinigte Staaten und 1870 in Roanoke Township, Randolph, Arkansas, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. Er starb am 7. Januar 1871 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Ravenden Springs, Lawrence, Arkansas, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Janes Cemetery, Ravenden Springs, Lawrence, Arkansas, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Bailey è nato il nel 1814 nel Carolina del Sud, Stati Uniti d'America, il suo padre, John T Bailey, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Jemima Russell, aveva 41 anni. Lui sposato Elisabeth Land il 15 luglio 1831, nel Carolina del Sud, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 7 figlie. Vissuto a DeKalb, Alabama, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1850 e nel Roanoke Township, Randolph, Arkansas, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1870. Morì il 7 gennaio 1871, nel Ravenden Springs, Lawrence, Arkansas, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 57 anni, e fu sepolto a Janes Cemetery, Ravenden Springs, Lawrence, Arkansas, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: John Baileyが1814年にアメリカ合衆国サウスカロライナ州で生まれた時，父John T Baileyは28歳，母Jemima Russellは41歳であった。1831年7月15日にアメリカ合衆国サウスカロライナ州でElisabeth Landと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1850年にはアメリカ合衆国アラバマ州デカルブに，1870年にはアメリカ合衆国アーカンソー州ランドルフロアノク トウンシュイップに住んでいた。彼は1871年1月7日にアメリカ合衆国アーカンソー州ラウレンスラベンデン スプリングズで57歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国アーカンソー州ラウレンスラベンデン スプリングズジャンス セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Roanoke Township, Randolph, Arkansas, Un: 미국 아칸소 주 랜돌프 로노크 타운시프
ko.Benton Township, Fulton, Arkansas, Unite: 미국 아칸소 주 프턴 벤턴 타운시프

ko.Janes Cemetery, Ravenden Springs, Randol: 미국 아칸소 주 로렌스 레이븐든 스프링스 제네스 세멧리
ko.lifeSummary: John Bailey(이)가 1814년 미국 사우스캐롤라이나 주에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John T Bailey(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Jemima Russell(은)는 41세였다. 그는 1831년 7월 15일 미국 사우스캐롤라이나 주에서 Elisabeth Land(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 1850년도에 미국 앨라배마 주 데캘브에서, 1870년도에 미국 아칸소 주 랜돌프 로노크 타운시프에서 살았다. 그는 1871년 1월 7일 미국 아칸소 주 로렌스 레이븐든 스프링스에서 57세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 아칸소 주 로렌스 레이븐든 스프링스 제네스 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Bailey nasceu em 1814, em Carolina do Sul, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, John T Bailey, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Jemima Russell, tinha 41 anos. Ele casou-se com Elisabeth Land em 15 de julho de 1831, em Carolina do Sul, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 7 filhas. Ele viveu em DeKalb, Alabama, Estados Unidos da América em 1850 e em Roanoke Township, Randolph, Arcansas, Estados Unidos da América em 1870. Ele faleceu em 7 de janeiro de 1871, em Ravenden Springs, Lawrence, Arcansas, Estados Unidos da América, com 57 anos, e foi sepultado em Janes Cemetery, Ravenden Springs, Lawrence, Arcansas, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Bailey родился в 1814 г. в местности Южная Каролина, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени John T Bailey было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Jemima Russell, 41. Он женился на женщине по имени Elisabeth Land 15 июля 1831 г. в местности Южная Каролина, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 7 дочерей. Он жил в местности декалб, Алабама, Соединенные штаты в 1850 году и в местности роанок тоуншип, рандолф, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты в 1870 году. Он умер 7 января 1871 г. в местности равенден спрингс, ларенс, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 57 лет и был похоронен в местности дженесь кеметири, равенден спрингс, ларенс, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Bailey nació en 1814, en Carolina del Sur, Estados Unidos, su papá, John T Bailey, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Jemima Russell, tenía 41 años. Se casó con Elisabeth Land el 15 de julio de 1831, en Carolina del Sur, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 7 hijas. Vivió en DeKalb, Alabama, Estados Unidos en 1850 y en Roanoke Township, Randolph, Arkansas, Estados Unidos en 1870. Murió el 7 de enero de 1871, en Ravenden Springs, Lawrence, Arkansas, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 57 años, y fue sepultado en Janes Cemetery, Ravenden Springs, Lawrence, Arkansas, Estados Unidos.
